name: Christmas Stollen
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour milk into a saucepan. Heat to scalding.
2. Turn off heat; stir in 1/2 cup of the granulated sugar and the salt. Let mixture cool to lukewarm.
3. In a large bowl, dissolve yeast in the 1/4 cup warm water; let mixture rest for 5 minutes.
4. Pour lukewarm milk mixture into yeast solution. Stir in 1 cup of the flour.
5. Beat dough with an electric mixer or egg beater until smooth. Cover bowl with cloth towel; let dough rise in a warm place 1 hour and 30 minutes or until double in bulk.
6. Punch down dough in bowl; fold in citron, cherries, almonds, lemon rind, and raisins. Add eggs, 3/4 cup of the softened butter, and nutmeg. Stir in 3 more cups flour; mix dough until smooth.
7. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead, working in enough of the remaining flour to make dough smooth and elastic.
8. Divide dough into halves; roll each portion into an oval, about 1/2 inch thick.
9. In a small saucepan, melt remaining butter; brush over ovals.
10. In a small bowl, combine cinnamon with the remaining 2 Tablespoons granulated sugar; sprinkle mixture over ovals.
11. Fold ovals in half, lengthwise. Place them on buttered baking sheet.
12. Twist ends of each oval toward each other to form a crescent.
13. Loosely cover ovals with wax paper and cloth towel. Let Stollen rise in a warm place about 1 hour or until double in bulk.
14. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
15. Bake Stollen for 45 minutes or until golden.
16. In a small bowl, combine confectioners' sugar with enough hot water to make a thick icing. Dribble icing over hot Stollen; let cool before slicing. Makes 1 Stollen.
